control_description: Automated validation rules at point of data entry to verify format, range, and business rule compliance. Includes mandatory field checks, format validation, and cross-field logic.
Systematic profiling of data assets to measure quality dimensions (accuracy, completeness, validity). Generates quality scores and identifies anomalies.
Real-time dashboards displaying data quality metrics with threshold alerts. Enables proactive identification of quality degradation.
Formal workflow for identifying, logging, and correcting data quality issues. Includes root cause analysis and remediation tracking.
Governance framework for master data including golden source designation, stewardship assignments, and change management procedures.
Automated reconciliation of data across systems to identify inconsistencies. Includes exception reporting and resolution tracking.
Automated detection of duplicate records using matching algorithms. Includes merge/purge procedures with audit trail.
Monitoring of data refresh cycles to ensure timeliness. Alerts when data exceeds staleness thresholds.
Specific validation controls for data used in models and AI/ML systems. Includes completeness checks, distribution analysis, and drift detection.
Defined escalation paths for data quality issues based on severity and business impact. Includes SLAs for resolution.
Standardised consent collection process ensuring valid, informed, specific consent is obtained before data processing. Includes consent language review and approval.
Central repository recording all consent grants with timestamp, scope, version, and channel. Enables consent verification and audit.
Process enabling data subjects to withdraw consent easily. Includes propagation of withdrawal across systems and cessation of processing.
Formal intake process for DSR requests (access, rectification, erasure, portability). Includes identity verification and request logging.
Workflow for processing DSR requests within regulatory timeframes. Includes data location identification, extraction/deletion, and response generation.
Tracking and monitoring of DSR response times against regulatory deadlines. Alerts for approaching deadlines.
Formal assessment of privacy risks for new initiatives, systems, or processing activities. Required before implementation.
Classification of data assets by sensitivity level with appropriate labelling. Determines handling requirements.
Access to data restricted based on job role and need-to-know principle. Includes role definitions and access matrices.
Periodic review of user access rights to verify appropriateness. Managers certify continued need for access.
Enhanced controls for privileged/administrative access including just-in-time access, session recording, and approval workflows.
Encryption of sensitive data stored in databases, file systems, and backups using approved encryption standards.
Encryption of data during transmission using TLS/SSL. Includes certificate management and protocol version controls.
Assessment of data transfers outside UAE against PDPL requirements. Determines legal basis and required safeguards.
Implementation of approved contractual clauses for international data transfers providing adequate safeguards.
Registry of all cross-border data transfers including destination, legal basis, safeguards, and data categories.
Continuous monitoring of security events to detect potential breaches, unauthorized access, and anomalous activity.
Specific detection rules for data-related security events including mass data access, unusual export activity, and access from anomalous locations.
Documented procedures for responding to data incidents including containment, investigation, and remediation steps.
Process for notifying regulators and affected individuals of data breaches within required timeframes (72 hours for regulator).
Central register of all data breaches and near-misses including root cause, impact assessment, and lessons learned.
Technical controls to prevent unauthorized data exfiltration via email, web, removable media, and cloud services.
Security controls on endpoints (laptops, desktops, mobile) to prevent data leakage and unauthorized access.
Pre-contract assessment of vendor data protection capabilities including security controls, certifications, and privacy practices.
Contractual agreements with vendors specifying data protection obligations, processing instructions, and liability provisions.
Ongoing monitoring of vendor compliance with data protection requirements including periodic assessments and audit rights.
Contractual requirement for vendors to notify Bank of data incidents within specified timeframes.
Controls ensuring data is only used for specified, legitimate purposes. Includes usage logging and purpose verification.
Comprehensive logging of data access including who, what, when, and from where. Retained for audit and investigation purposes.
Technical controls to detect and prevent unauthorized modification of data including checksums, digital signatures, and change detection.
Use of synthetic/anonymised data for testing, development, and analytics to minimise exposure of real personal data.
Centrally managed templates for all consumer disclosures ensuring consistency and regulatory compliance. Includes version control and approval workflow.
Review process ensuring disclosure content is complete, accurate, and compliant before publication or distribution.
Process for maintaining accurate, complete fee schedules with change management and consumer notification procedures.
Standardised risk warning templates for products with capital-at-risk, ensuring consistent and prominent disclosure.
Review process specifically checking for potentially misleading statements, omissions, or implications in consumer communications.
Process ensuring all consumer disclosures are available in Arabic with accurate translation and cultural appropriateness.
Review ensuring disclosures use plain, understandable language avoiding jargon and complex terminology.
Review ensuring disclosures meet accessibility requirements for People of Determination including format, font size, and alternative formats.
Controls ensuring disclosure content is consistent across all channels (branch, online, mobile, call centre).
Checklist ensuring all required disclosures are provided before contract execution with documented acknowledgment.
Process ensuring consumers are informed of and able to exercise cooling-off rights with proper disclosure timing.
System for scheduling and tracking consumer notifications for changes (rates, terms, renewals) within required notice periods.
Tracking of notification delivery with confirmation and retry mechanisms for failed deliveries.
Systematic monitoring of regulatory developments (CBUAE, UAE Data Office) to identify changes affecting data management.
Periodic self-assessment of compliance with data protection and consumer protection regulations.
Scheduled review and update of data management policies to ensure continued relevance and regulatory alignment.
Training programs ensuring staff understand and can apply data management policies. Includes completion tracking.
Ongoing monitoring of policy compliance through metrics, spot checks, and exception tracking.
Process for requesting, approving, and tracking exceptions to data management policies with time limits and conditions.
Inventory of data records with classification for retention purposes. Maps records to retention schedules.
Automated enforcement of retention periods with alerts for records approaching end-of-life.
Procedures for secure destruction of data at end of retention period with certification and audit trail.
Process for placing and managing legal holds on data to prevent destruction during litigation or investigation.
Register documenting data owners for all critical data assets with accountability assignments.
Formal program assigning data stewards with defined responsibilities for data quality and governance within their domain.
Appointment of Data Protection Officer with direct reporting line to senior management as required by PDPL.
Data Steering Group oversight of data governance activities including review of metrics, issues, and strategic decisions.
Maintenance of enterprise data catalogue documenting data assets, definitions, lineage, and ownership.
Specific governance controls for data used in models and AI/ML systems including lineage, quality certification, and usage tracking.

Maintain an inventory of all spreadsheets, desktop databases, and end-user computing applications used in risk data aggregation. Apply EUC policy controls including version control, access restrictions, change logging, and periodic accuracy validation. Flag critical EUC applications for prioritised automation.

Automated reconciliation of aggregated risk data against authoritative source systems and accounting data. Includes tolerance-based exception reporting, break investigation workflows, and escalation to data quality team for material discrepancies.

Periodic testing of the bank's ability to produce critical risk data aggregations and reports within established stress/crisis timeframes. Includes simulation of ad hoc supervisory data requests, verification of intraday position reporting capability, and documentation of gaps with remediation timelines.

Automated monitoring of data production and aggregation timeliness against defined SLAs for all critical risk data feeds. Includes real-time alerting for SLA breaches, automated escalation for missed deadlines, and trend reporting to identify deteriorating data pipeline performance.

Maintain an enterprise-wide data dictionary and taxonomy with unified naming conventions, single identifiers for legal entities, counterparties, customers, and accounts. Enforce consistent data definitions across the banking group through automated validation against the dictionary during data integration.

Structured validation and sign-off process for risk management reports before distribution. Includes automated edit and reasonableness checks on quantitative data, manual review of qualitative interpretations, verification of forward-looking content, and formal sign-off by report owner.

Annual assessment with board and senior management to confirm that risk reports remain relevant, comprehensive, and appropriate for governance and decision-making. Includes review of report inventory against information needs, assessment of report clarity and usefulness, and identification of gaps in forward-looking and concentration risk content.
